name: Sierra Club Foundation
mission: The Sierra Club Foundation promotes climate solutions, conservation, and movement building through a powerful combination of strategic philanthropy and ...
theoryOfChange: The Sierra Club Foundation's website, specifically the 'The Foundation for a Healthy Planet' page, outlines their 'Goals & Impact' which include solving the climate crisis, securing land protections, getting people outdoors, building a movement, and shifting trillions for climate justice. It also details their 'Methods' for achieving these goals, such as partnering with donors to align financial resources with strategic outcomes and providing resources to the Sierra Club and other nonprofits for various programs. Furthermore, the 'SCF Organizational Assessment' indicates that the Foundation worked with the Sierra Club to develop a strategic framework and that its Catalytic Capital Portfolio supports programmatic efforts aligned with shared climate and social justice goals, including the Sierra Club's 2030 Strategic Framework. The Foundation also utilizes an 'Endowment Impact Benchmark' as a framework to assess its commitment to impact and sustainable investing.

scoreSummary: Sierra Club Foundation shows strong impact and moderate alignment.

evaluationSources: Platinum Rating (Endowment Impact Benchmark) (Intentional Endowments Network)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 1.3% of revenue ($1,401,675 on $108,188,731)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue >$50M, CEO pay above 1% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 21/50: LOW Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.evidence: Board: 30 members, but For orgs with revenue >$50M, CEO pay above 1% of revenue warrants scrutiny. — oversight concern
components.name: Governance

judge_issues.message: Website shows related EIN (94-1350342), likely parent/subsidiary of 94-6069890

risks.description: CEO compensation is 1.3% of revenue ($1,401,675 on $108,188,731)

score_details.score_summary: Sierra Club Foundation shows strong impact and moderate alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sierra_Club_Foundation)

all_citations.claim: The Sierra Club Foundation promotes climate solutions and conservation through strategic philanthropy.
The organization reported total revenue of $108,188,731, a program expense ratio of 86.0%, and 17.7 months of working capital.
The foundation offers multiple ways to give for donors interested in supporting environmental causes.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization demonstrates exceptional financial health and efficiency, though its alignment score reflects its status as a general advocacy group rather than one tailored to specific religious donor needs.
areas_for_improvement: Limited direct alignment with specific Muslim donor preferences or community-focused outcomes
High reliance on advocacy-based impact which can be harder to measure than direct service delivery

dimension_explanations.alignment: While the cause of climate urgency is high, the alignment score is moderate because the organization focuses on broad environmental advocacy rather than specific Muslim community needs [1]. Donors can support these efforts through various sadaqah-eligible giving methods, though it lacks a specialized track record for Muslim donor fit [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation achieves a high impact score by directing 86.0% of its $108,188,731 revenue toward core programs like the Beyond Coal Campaign and Outdoors for All [1][2]. Its financial stability is evidenced by 17.7 months of working capital, providing a strong capacity-building foundation for long-term environmental advocacy [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Sierra Club Foundation serves as a strategic philanthropic engine for global climate solutions and the protection of wild spaces.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 86.0% ensuring most funds reach environmental initiatives
Strong fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.11 per $1 raised
Robust liquidity with 17.7 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: The Sierra Club Foundation promotes climate solutions, conservation, and movement building through strategic philanthropy and advocacy programs [1]. In the 2023 fiscal year, the organization reported a total revenue of $108,188,731 to support its environmental mission [2].

all_citations.quote: Your gift to the Sierra Club Foundation is tax-deductible
all_citations.source_name: Sierra Club Foundation - Ways to Give

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization demonstrates exceptional financial health and efficiency, though its alignment score reflects its status as a general advocacy group rather than one tailored to specific religious donor needs.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization has experienced a <cite id="16">3-year revenue CAGR of -9.5%</cite>, indicating a recent downward trend in annual funding.
While the Foundation tracks outcomes, it currently lacks <cite id="17">consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for cost-per-beneficiary analysis.

bbb_assessment.summary: Does not currently meet BBB Wise Giving Alliance Standards for Charity Accountability.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The Foundation maintains massive reserves to buffer against revenue fluctuations.
risk_factors: <cite id="24">CEO compensation of $322,306</cite> and total officer pay exceeding 1% of revenue
<cite id="20">Non-compliance with BBB Wise Giving Alliance standards</cite>
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="16">declining revenue trends</cite> and <cite id="24">high executive compensation</cite> relative to revenue for an organization of this size.

confidence.notes: Strong financial and governance data available.

alignment.explanation: The charity is <cite id="9">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE</cite> and addresses the <cite id="22">urgent cause of climate advocacy</cite>, though it lacks specific Muslim community programming.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be improved by <cite id="23">clearly stating the organization's stance on zakat-eligibility</cite> or creating dedicated pathways for faith-based giving.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this institution due to its <cite id="1">66-year track record</cite>, <cite id="12">Platinum Candid status</cite>, and <cite id="19">100% independent board</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should address the <cite id="20">0/20 standards met</cite> status currently listed by the BBB Wise Giving Alliance.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by <cite id="15">massive grantmaking capacity</cite> and a <cite id="21">clear theory of change</cite> focused on resource-efficient clean energy.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="17">publishing consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to assess cost-effectiveness.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade B - Documented Theory of Change with tracked outcomes

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Provides fiscal support and grants to the Sierra Club and affiliated environmental movements.

rich_narrative.headline: The Sierra Club Foundation serves as a strategic philanthropic engine for global climate solutions and the protection of wild spaces.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize long-term environmental sustainability and want to fund large-scale advocacy and legal movement-building in the US.
donor_motivations: Value systemic change over individual direct aid
Care about climate crisis and clean energy transition
Prefer established institutions with significant financial reserves
Want to support environmental justice through strategic grantmaking
giving_considerations: Donations support advocacy which may have delayed or indirect impact
The organization acts as a fiscal engine for the broader Sierra Club movement
Recent revenue trends show a contraction in annual funding
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those looking for immediate, direct humanitarian relief like food or medical aid.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization has a <cite id="21">clear theory of change</cite> and <cite id="26">tracks specific outcome metrics</cite>, though it lacks independent RCT evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The Foundation funds a transition to a resource-efficient clean energy economy while securing protections for public lands and waters.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence in advocacy ensures that philanthropic capital is effectively influencing policy and conservation outcomes.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While assets are high, the <cite id="16">negative revenue growth</cite> suggests a need for new donor acquisition to maintain grantmaking levels.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="11">exceptionally high working capital</cite> compared to the <cite id="25">80.0% peer program ratio median</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focus on scientific research and policy
similar_organizations.name: Union of Concerned Scientists

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="10">program expense ratio of 86.0%</cite>, which is consistent with its role as a major grantmaker.
The Foundation holds <cite id="11">17.7 months of working capital</cite>, providing significant financial stability for long-term advocacy campaigns.
The charity has earned a <cite id="12">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, the highest level of disclosure available.

strengths_deep_dive: The Sierra Club Foundation's financial health is anchored by its massive asset base, with <cite id="13">total assets of $199,302,924</cite> reported in 2023. This allows the organization to act as a stable fiscal sponsor for numerous environmental projects. Its <cite id="14">independent board of 30 members</cite> provides extensive oversight for its complex grantmaking activities, which included <cite id="15">$281,217,080 in total grants</cite> distributed to domestic recipients, primarily the Sierra Club's operational arms.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1960</cite>, the Sierra Club Foundation serves as a strategic philanthropic engine for global climate solutions and the protection of wild spaces. The organization operates primarily as a <cite id="2">grantmaking entity</cite>, distributing significant funds to support environmental advocacy and conservation efforts. In the 2023 fiscal year, the Foundation reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $89,068,739</cite>, derived largely from <cite id="4">$85,543,074 in contributions</cite>. Its primary mission involves <cite id="5">promoting climate solutions and movement building</cite> through a combination of strategic philanthropy and direct program support. The Foundation's work is centered in the <cite id="6">United States</cite>, where it funds diverse initiatives including <cite id="7">Clean Power Plan Advocacy and Nature Protection</cite>. Despite its large scale, the organization maintains a <cite id="8">fundraising efficiency of $0.11 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor contributions are directed toward its environmental objectives rather than administrative overhead. As a <cite id="9">SADAQAH-ELIGIBLE</cite> organization, it offers a pathway for Muslim donors to contribute to long-term ecological sustainability and climate justice.
